text.title: Substack vs Beehiiv vs ConvertKit vs Ghost Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of substack-beehiiv-pick enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Recommendation cites at least 3 of the 4 weighted criteria in the rationale.
rule.rationale: Single-criterion recommendations (e.g., 'cheapest') are brittle; multi-criterion forces real reasoning.

rule.statement: Owned-audience priority ≥4 → Beehiiv or Ghost; ≤2 → Substack or ConvertKit allowed.
rule.rationale: Substack owns the brand surface; Ghost / Beehiiv give the author the domain + list export.

rule.statement: Sub revenue intent + low tech tolerance → Substack; sponsorship-heavy + scale → Beehiiv.
rule.rationale: Substack handles paid subs natively; Beehiiv has Boosts for sponsor matching.

rule.statement: Migration cost is calculated and shown: subscriber export fee + DNS swap + URL rewrite + ~10% list attrition.
rule.rationale: Authors underestimate migration cost; explicit number prevents impulse switching later.

rule.statement: Decision record is versioned at `marketing/decisions/newsletter-platform.md` with date and signature.
rule.rationale: Decisions without versioned record get re-litigated; signed records compress argument.
